file.content:
services:
  traefik:
    image: traefik:v3.3
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "8080:80"
      - "8443:443"
    volumes:
      - traefik-data:/data
      - ./.local/certs:/data/certs
    command:
      - "--entrypoints.web.address=:80"
      - "--entrypoints.websecure.address=:443"
      - "--providers.file.directory=/data/dynamic"
      - "--providers.file.watch=true"
      - "--log.level=INFO"
    networks:
      - sandcastle-web
    depends_on:
      init-traefik:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  init-traefik:
    image: busybox
    volumes:
      - traefik-data:/data
    command:
      - sh
      - -c
      - |
        mkdir -p /data/dynamic /data/certs
        chown -R 220568:220568 /data

  postgres:
    image: postgres:18
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql
      - ./docker/postgres/init-databases.sh:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init-databases.sh:ro
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: sandcastle
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
      POSTGRES_DB: sandcastle_production
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U sandcastle -d sandcastle_production"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  init-data:
    image: busybox
    volumes:
      - sandcastle-data:/data
    command: sh -c "mkdir -p /data/users /data/sandboxes && chown -R 220568:220568 /data"

  web:
    image: sandcastle:local
    build:
      context: .
      args:
        BUILD_VERSION: "${BUILD_VERSION:-dev}"
        BUILD_GIT_SHA: "${BUILD_GIT_SHA:-local}"
        BUILD_GIT_DIRTY: "${BUILD_GIT_DIRTY:-false}"
        BUILD_DATE: "${BUILD_DATE:-$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)}"
    container_name: sandcastle-web
    command: ["./bin/rails", "server", "-b", "0.0.0.0", "-p", "80"]
    group_add:
      - "${DOCKER_GID:-0}"
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      - sandcastle-data:/data
      - traefik-data:/data/traefik
      - ./.local/certs:/data/traefik/certs
    networks:
      - default
      - sandcastle-web
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: production
      RAILS_SHOW_DETAILED_ERRORS: "true"
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      SANDCASTLE_HOST: dev.sand
      SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR: /data
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: mkcert
      SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL: https://dev.sand:8443
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      init-data:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  worker:
    image: sandcastle:local
    build:
      context: .
      args:
        BUILD_VERSION: "${BUILD_VERSION:-dev}"
        BUILD_GIT_SHA: "${BUILD_GIT_SHA:-local}"
        BUILD_GIT_DIRTY: "${BUILD_GIT_DIRTY:-false}"
        BUILD_DATE: "${BUILD_DATE:-$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)}"
    container_name: sandcastle-worker
    command: ["bundle", "exec", "rake", "solid_queue:start"]
    group_add:
      - "${DOCKER_GID:-0}"
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      - sandcastle-data:/data
      - traefik-data:/data/traefik
    networks:
      - default
      - sandcastle-web
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: production
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      SANDCASTLE_HOST: dev.sand
      SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR: /data
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: mkcert
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      init-data:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  migrate:
    image: sandcastle:local
    command: ["./bin/rails", "db:prepare"]
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: production
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER: thies
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_EMAIL: thieso@gmail.com
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD: tubu
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_SSH_KEY: "ssh-ed25519 REDACTED thieso@gmail.com"
      SANDCASTLE_SKIP_PASSWORD_CHANGE: "true"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy

networks:
  sandcastle-web:
    external: true

volumes:
  pgdata:
  sandcastle-data:
  traefik-data:

file.content:
services:
  traefik:
    image: traefik:v3.3
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "8080:80"
      - "8443:443"
    volumes:
      - traefik-data:/data
      - ./.cert:/certs:ro
    command:
      - "--entrypoints.web.address=:80"
      - "--entrypoints.websecure.address=:443"
      - "--providers.file.directory=/data/dynamic"
      - "--providers.file.watch=true"
      - "--log.level=INFO"
    networks:
      - sandcastle-web
    depends_on:
      init-traefik:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  init-traefik:
    image: busybox
    volumes:
      - traefik-data:/data
    command:
      - sh
      - -c
      - |
        mkdir -p /data/dynamic
        chown -R 220568:220568 /data
        cat > /data/dynamic/rails.yml << 'EOFCONFIG'
        http:
          routers:
            rails-http:
              rule: "Host(`${DEV_HOST}`)"
              service: rails
              entryPoints:
                - web
            rails-https:
              rule: "Host(`${DEV_HOST}`)"
              service: rails
              entryPoints:
                - websecure
              tls: {}
          services:
            rails:
              loadBalancer:
                servers:
                  - url: "http://sandcastle-web:80"
        EOFCONFIG

  postgres:
    image: postgres:18
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql
      - ./docker/postgres/init-databases.sh:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init-databases.sh:ro
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: sandcastle
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
      POSTGRES_DB: sandcastle_development
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U sandcastle -d sandcastle_development"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  init-data:
    image: busybox
    volumes:
      - sandcastle-data:/data
    command: sh -c "mkdir -p /data/users /data/sandboxes && chown -R 220568:220568 /data"

  web:
    image: sandcastle:dev
    build:
      context: .
      target: development
      args:
        BUILD_VERSION: "dev"
        BUILD_GIT_SHA: ""
        BUILD_GIT_DIRTY: ""
        BUILD_DATE: ""
    container_name: sandcastle-web
    group_add:
      - "${DOCKER_GID:-0}"
    volumes:
      # Mount source code for live reloading
      - .:/rails
      # Persist gems in volume (faster than bind mount)
      - bundle-data:/usr/local/bundle
      # Persist node modules
      - node-modules:/rails/node_modules
      # Share Docker socket
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      # Data directory
      - sandcastle-data:/data
      # Traefik config
      - traefik-data:/data/traefik
    networks:
      - default
      - sandcastle-web
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: development
      PORT: 80
      BINDING: 0.0.0.0
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      SANDCASTLE_HOST: ${DEV_HOST}
      SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR: /data
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: selfsigned
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR: /certs
      SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL: https://${DEV_HOST}:8443
      SANDCASTLE_VNC_URL: https://${DEV_HOST}:8443
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
    restart: unless-stopped
    command: ./bin/dev
    depends_on:
      init-data:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      init-traefik:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  worker:
    image: sandcastle:dev
    build:
      context: .
      target: development
      args:
        BUILD_VERSION: "dev"
        BUILD_GIT_SHA: ""
        BUILD_GIT_DIRTY: ""
        BUILD_DATE: ""
    container_name: sandcastle-dev-worker
    group_add:
      - "${DOCKER_GID:-0}"
    volumes:
      # Mount source code for live reloading
      - .:/rails
      # Persist gems in volume (faster than bind mount)
      - bundle-data:/usr/local/bundle
      # Share Docker socket
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      # Data directory
      - sandcastle-data:/data
      # Traefik config
      - traefik-data:/data/traefik
    networks:
      - default
      - sandcastle-web
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: development
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      SANDCASTLE_HOST: ${DEV_HOST}
      SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR: /data
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: selfsigned
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR: /certs
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
    restart: unless-stopped
    command: ["bundle", "exec", "rake", "solid_queue:start"]
    depends_on:
      init-data:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  migrate:
    image: sandcastle:dev
    build:
      context: .
      target: development
    volumes:
      - .:/rails
      - bundle-data:/usr/local/bundle
    command: ["./bin/rails", "db:prepare"]
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: development
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER: thies
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_EMAIL: thieso@gmail.com
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD: tubu
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_SSH_KEY: "ssh-ed25519 REDACTED thieso@gmail.com"
      SANDCASTLE_SKIP_PASSWORD_CHANGE: "true"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy

networks:
  sandcastle-web:
    external: true

volumes:
  bundle-data:
  node-modules:
  pgdata:
  sandcastle-data:
  traefik-data:

I can pre-publish the port range (3000-3099) and configure Traefik through command-line arguments instead.

Wait, that won't work because Traefik needs to know about the entrypoints before it can route to them. When the dynamic config tries to use `tcp-3042`, Traefik will ignore it if that entrypoint wasn't declared statically. So I need to either pre-declare all 100 ports in the command flags or use a static config file that gets updated. The config file approach is cleaner and more maintainable, so I'll restructure the Traefik setup to have an init script write the static config to a mounted volume.

The plan is to mount the traefik-data volume at `/etc/traefik` in Traefik so it reads the static config from there, keep certificates at `./.local/certs` bound to `/etc/traefik/certs`, and mount the same volume at `/data/traefik` in the web and worker services so Rails can write updates to it. This way Traefik reads from `/etc/traefik/traefik.yml` and the dynamic config from `/etc/traefik/dynamic/`, while Rails writes to the same files through the `/data/traefik` mount point.

Now I'm setting up the docker-compose configuration with Traefik pulling from the mounted volume, keeping certificates separate and read-only, and adding the init-traefik service as a dependency. I'm checking the existing docker-compose.local.yml to see if the traefik-data volume mount is already configured for the web container, and it looks like it is — the Rails app can write directly to `/data/traefik/traefik.yml` through that existing mount.

The tricky part is that if I mount `traefik-data:/etc/traefik` in the Traefik service, the same file would be at `/etc/traefik/traefik.yml` there, and I need to be careful about the certs directory since `./.local/certs:/data/traefik/certs` would overlap with the `traefik-data:/data/traefik` mount that's already in place.

Looking at the current Traefik setup, it mounts `traefik-data:/data` and `./.local/certs:/data/certs`, with the command pointing to `--providers.file.directory=/data/dynamic`. If I change the mount point to `/etc/traefik`, I'd need to update that path to `/etc/traefik/dynamic` as well.

I'm also realizing the `traefik-data` volume might already contain data from a previous `init-traefik` run, so changing the mount point from `/data` to `/etc/traefik` would shift where those directories live within the volume—but that should be fine since the structure just moves to a different path.

Now I need to write out the full updated docker-compose.local.yml with the static config file approach, TCP port range, container names, and environment variables for web and worker services. So the provider path works perfectly with this volume setup, and the traefik.yml file will be accessible to all containers at their respective mount points. I just need to update init-traefik to actually generate that configuration file.

For the TLS setup, Rails writes the tls.yml config to the dynamic directory, and the certificates themselves are mounted from `./.local/certs` into the web container where Rails can reference them relative to the SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR environment variable.

Looking at the docker-compose.local.yml, I see that SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE is set to mkcert on the web service, but SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR isn't explicitly configured, so it defaults to `/data/certs`. However, the actual certs are mounted at `/data/traefik/certs`, and the route_manager.rb code is looking for them at the default `/data/certs` path—so there's a mismatch between where Rails expects the certs and where they're actually mounted.

That's a separate issue though. For this PR, I need to focus on the TCP changes: add the container name to both compose files, configure the TCP port range, set the necessary environment variables, and switch Traefik from using command-line arguments to a static config file so the entrypoint script can modify it.

Now I'll update the docker-compose.local.yml with just the essential changes.

Wait, I should verify the volume mount paths first. In docker-compose.dev.yml, the traefik-data volume is mounted at `/data/traefik` in the web container, so `REDACTED.yml` would work there. Let me check what the docker-compose.local.yml setup looks like...

It's the same volume mount path. Perfect. For the Traefik container itself, I'll mount that same volume at `/etc/traefik` so it can read the config file from its default location. Now I'm ready to write out the updated compose files.

content.text:
Both files need the same three changes:
1. `container_name: sandcastle-traefik` (so `Docker::Container.get` finds it)
2. TCP port range published
3. Switch Traefik from `command:` flags to a static config file so `ensure_tcp_entrypoint` can write to it at runtime

The local setups use `command:` flags instead of `traefik.yml`, which means `ensure_tcp_entrypoint` would silently skip (file doesn't exist). I'll have `init-traefik` write the initial `traefik.yml` and mount the volume at `/etc/traefik` in Traefik.

input.content:
services:
  traefik:
    image: traefik:v3.3
    container_name: sandcastle-traefik
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "8080:80"
      - "8443:443"
      - "3000-3099:3000-3099"
    volumes:
      - traefik-data:/etc/traefik
      - ./.local/certs:/etc/traefik/certs:ro
    networks:
      - sandcastle-web
    depends_on:
      init-traefik:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  init-traefik:
    image: busybox
    volumes:
      - traefik-data:/data
    command:
      - sh
      - -c
      - |
        mkdir -p /data/dynamic /data/certs
        chown -R 220568:220568 /data
        if [ ! -f /data/traefik.yml ]; then
          cat > /data/traefik.yml << 'EOF'
        entryPoints:
          web:
            address: ":80"
          websecure:
            address: ":443"
        providers:
          file:
            directory: /etc/traefik/dynamic
            watch: true
        log:
          level: INFO
        EOF
        fi

  postgres:
    image: postgres:18
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql
      - ./docker/postgres/init-databases.sh:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init-databases.sh:ro
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: sandcastle
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
      POSTGRES_DB: sandcastle_production
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U sandcastle -d sandcastle_production"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  init-data:
    image: busybox
    volumes:
      - sandcastle-data:/data
    command: sh -c "mkdir -p /data/users /data/sandboxes && chown -R 220568:220568 /data"

  web:
    image: sandcastle:local
    build:
      context: .
      args:
        BUILD_VERSION: "${BUILD_VERSION:-dev}"
        BUILD_GIT_SHA: "${BUILD_GIT_SHA:-local}"
        BUILD_GIT_DIRTY: "${BUILD_GIT_DIRTY:-false}"
        BUILD_DATE: "${BUILD_DATE:-$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)}"
    container_name: sandcastle-web
    command: ["./bin/rails", "server", "-b", "0.0.0.0", "-p", "80"]
    group_add:
      - "${DOCKER_GID:-0}"
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      - sandcastle-data:/data
      - traefik-data:/data/traefik
      - ./.local/certs:/data/traefik/certs
    networks:
      - default
      - sandcastle-web
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: production
      RAILS_SHOW_DETAILED_ERRORS: "true"
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      SANDCASTLE_HOST: dev.sand
      SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR: /data
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: mkcert
      SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL: https://dev.sand:8443
      SANDCASTLE_TRAEFIK_CONFIG: /data/traefik/traefik.yml
      SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MIN: "3000"
      SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MAX: "3099"
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      init-data:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  worker:
    image: sandcastle:local
    build:
      context: .
      args:
        BUILD_VERSION: "${BUILD_VERSION:-dev}"
        BUILD_GIT_SHA: "${BUILD_GIT_SHA:-local}"
        BUILD_GIT_DIRTY: "${BUILD_GIT_DIRTY:-false}"
        BUILD_DATE: "${BUILD_DATE:-$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)}"
    container_name: sandcastle-worker
    command: ["bundle", "exec", "rake", "solid_queue:start"]
    group_add:
      - "${DOCKER_GID:-0}"
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      - sandcastle-data:/data
      - traefik-data:/data/traefik
    networks:
      - default
      - sandcastle-web
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: production
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      SANDCASTLE_HOST: dev.sand
      SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR: /data
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: mkcert
      SANDCASTLE_TRAEFIK_CONFIG: /data/traefik/traefik.yml
      SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MIN: "3000"
      SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MAX: "3099"
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      init-data:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  migrate:
    image: sandcastle:local
    command: ["./bin/rails", "db:prepare"]
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: production
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER: thies
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_EMAIL: thieso@gmail.com
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD: tubu
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_SSH_KEY: "ssh-ed25519 REDACTED thieso@gmail.com"
      SANDCASTLE_SKIP_PASSWORD_CHANGE: "true"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy

networks:
  sandcastle-web:
    external: true

volumes:
  pgdata:
  sandcastle-data:
  traefik-data:

input.content:
services:
  traefik:
    image: traefik:v3.3
    container_name: sandcastle-traefik
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "8080:80"
      - "8443:443"
      - "3000-3099:3000-3099"
    volumes:
      - traefik-data:/etc/traefik
      - ./.cert:/etc/traefik/certs:ro
    networks:
      - sandcastle-web
    depends_on:
      init-traefik:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  init-traefik:
    image: busybox
    volumes:
      - traefik-data:/data
    command:
      - sh
      - -c
      - |
        mkdir -p /data/dynamic
        chown -R 220568:220568 /data
        if [ ! -f /data/traefik.yml ]; then
          cat > /data/traefik.yml << 'EOF'
        entryPoints:
          web:
            address: ":80"
          websecure:
            address: ":443"
        providers:
          file:
            directory: /etc/traefik/dynamic
            watch: true
        log:
          level: INFO
        EOF
          cat > /data/dynamic/rails.yml << 'EOFCONFIG'
        http:
          routers:
            rails-http:
              rule: "Host(`${DEV_HOST}`)"
              service: rails
              entryPoints:
                - web
            rails-https:
              rule: "Host(`${DEV_HOST}`)"
              service: rails
              entryPoints:
                - websecure
              tls: {}
          services:
            rails:
              loadBalancer:
                servers:
                  - url: "http://sandcastle-web:80"
        EOFCONFIG
        fi

  postgres:
    image: postgres:18
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql
      - ./docker/postgres/init-databases.sh:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init-databases.sh:ro
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: sandcastle
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
      POSTGRES_DB: sandcastle_development
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U sandcastle -d sandcastle_development"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  init-data:
    image: busybox
    volumes:
      - sandcastle-data:/data
    command: sh -c "mkdir -p /data/users /data/sandboxes && chown -R 220568:220568 /data"

  web:
    image: sandcastle:dev
    build:
      context: .
      target: development
      args:
        BUILD_VERSION: "dev"
        BUILD_GIT_SHA: ""
        BUILD_GIT_DIRTY: ""
        BUILD_DATE: ""
    container_name: sandcastle-web
    group_add:
      - "${DOCKER_GID:-0}"
    volumes:
      # Mount source code for live reloading
      - .:/rails
      # Persist gems in volume (faster than bind mount)
      - bundle-data:/usr/local/bundle
      # Persist node modules
      - node-modules:/rails/node_modules
      # Share Docker socket
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      # Data directory
      - sandcastle-data:/data
      # Traefik config
      - traefik-data:/data/traefik
    networks:
      - default
      - sandcastle-web
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: development
      PORT: 80
      BINDING: 0.0.0.0
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      SANDCASTLE_HOST: ${DEV_HOST}
      SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR: /data
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: selfsigned
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR: /certs
      SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL: https://${DEV_HOST}:8443
      SANDCASTLE_VNC_URL: https://${DEV_HOST}:8443

content.text:
The key structural change: Traefik's volume now mounts at `/etc/traefik` (its default config directory) instead of `/data`. That means:

| Container | Mount | Sees `traefik.yml` at |
|-----------|-------|----------------------|
| Traefik | `traefik-data:/etc/traefik` | `/etc/traefik/traefik.yml` ← read on start |
| web/worker | `traefik-data:/data/traefik` | `/data/traefik/traefik.yml` ← `SANDCASTLE_TRAEFIK_CONFIG` |

Same volume, different paths — same file. When `ensure_tcp_entrypoint` writes to `/data/traefik/traefik.yml` and restarts the Traefik container, Traefik boots and reads the updated config from `/etc/traefik/traefik.yml`.

One thing to note: if you have an existing `traefik-data` volume from before this change, it has the old structure (`/data/dynamic` inside it). You'll need to remove and recreate it:

```bash
docker compose -f docker-compose.local.yml down
docker volume rm sandcastle_traefik-data   # or whatever the project-prefixed name is
docker compose -f docker-compose.local.yml up -d
```

The `init-traefik` `if [ ! -f /data/traefik.yml ]` guard means it won't overwrite on restarts once the file exists (so `ensure_tcp_entrypoint`'s additions survive container restarts).

message.content: how different are the deploy:local and ./installer.sh install structures?

content.thinking:
The user is asking about the structural differences between the `deploy:local` setup (docker-compose.local.yml) and the production installer setup (installer.sh / docker-compose.yml.template). Let me look at both and compare them to give a clear answer.

Key differences I can see already:
1. Traefik config: local uses volume mount + init-traefik to write traefik.yml; installer uses bind-mount of pre-generated traefik.yml
2. Data storage: local uses named volumes; installer uses bind mounts to /sandcastle/data
3. Postgres: installer has a specific init-databases.sh for multiple databases


4. Web container: local builds from source; installer uses pre-built image
5. Environment: local has hardcoded dev credentials; installer reads from .env file
6. Traefik TLS: local uses mkcert certs; installer can be letsencrypt/selfsigned/mkcert

Now I'm examining the actual installer-generated docker-compose.yml to see how the data and Traefik configuration are structured in production.

The installer approach uses bind mounts for data from `/sandcastle/data/` on the host, with a pre-generated `traefik.yml` file that gets mounted directly into the Traefik container. It relies on pre-built images from a registry and pulls environment variables from a `.env` file. The local setup, by contrast, uses named Docker volumes for persistence and has an init container that generates the Traefik config into a volume at runtime. The core differences come down to how data persists—bind mounts versus named volumes—and how the Traefik static configuration gets provisioned, whether pre-generated or dynamically created.

Now I'm realizing that since I've aligned the local setup to also use static config, the Traefik configuration approach is actually closer between the two. But the fundamental divergence remains in data storage strategy and how the static config file arrives in the container. The user might be questioning whether these differences are justified or if we should consolidate the approaches further.

content.text: Let me look at both side by side properly:

content.thinking:
Now I have a clear view of both. Let me compare them:

**Installer (production) - docker-compose.yml.template**:
- Traefik: `traefik.yml` pre-generated by installer, bind-mounted at `/etc/traefik/traefik.yml`. Dynamic configs at `/data/dynamic:ro`, certs at `/data/certs:ro`, acme.json at `/data/acme.json`
- All data via bind mounts to host filesystem
- No `init-traefik` service needed - installer pre-creates all files
- `runtime: runc` on all services


- `migrate` service handled by the app container
- No `init-data` service - installer pre-creates directories

**deploy:local (docker-compose.local.yml)**:
- Traefik: config written by `init-traefik` init container to named volume, mounted at `/etc/traefik`
- All data via named Docker volumes
- Has `init-traefik` and `init-data` init services
- No `runtime:` specified
- Local build image

The key distinction is how Traefik gets configured: the installer pre-generates `traefik.yml` on the host filesystem, while the local setup uses an init container to write it to a volume at runtime. castle/data/traefik/traefik.yml` BEFORE starting docker-compose. Rails mounts the entire data directory (`/data:/data`), so it can write to `/data/traefik/traefik.yml` directly. Traefik gets the same file via a specific file bind-mount at `/etc/traefik/traefik.yml`.

- **deploy:local**: No host filesystem for data - uses named volumes. `init-traefik` writes the initial `traefik.yml` into the volume. Traefik reads from the volume at `/etc/traefik`. Rails reads/writes via the same volume at `/data/traefik`.

The **dynamic config path** is also different:
- Installer: `${DATA_MOUNT}/traefik/dynamic:/data/dynamic:ro` - dynamic configs at `/data/dynamic` in Traefik container

Now I'm seeing the path mismatch in the local setup: Rails always writes dynamic configs to `/data/traefik/dynamic/`, which works fine in the installer since both containers see the same host bind-mount. But in the local deployment with named volumes, Traefik is reading from `/etc/traefik/dynamic/` while Rails is writing to `/data/traefik/dynamic/` in the same volume—they're pointing to different locations within the container's view.

The core issue is that the static config setup diverged between the two deployments, and `ensure_tcp_entrypoint` needs to account for both the file-specific bind-mount approach (installer) and the full-volume mount approach (local). The `SANDCASTLE_TRAEFIK_CONFIG` environment variable is already set up to handle this distinction. The key differences boil down to storage approach and initialization strategy: the installer uses host bind mounts with pre-generated files for production reliability, while the local setup leverages named volumes and init containers for development convenience. These architectural choices make sense for their respective contexts.

content.text:
The differences are meaningful but there's one important inconsistency introduced by our changes. Here's the comparison:

**Structural differences (intentional, appropriate)**:

| | Installer | deploy:local |
|---|---|---|
| Data storage | Host bind-mounts (`/sandcastle/data/…`) | Named Docker volumes |
| Bootstrap | Installer pre-creates all files before compose starts | `init-traefik` / `init-data` init containers write at first boot |
| Images | Registry pull | Local build |
| `runtime:` | `runc` on all services | Not set |
| Encryption keys | Required (`AR_ENCRYPTION_*`) | Skipped (dev) |

**The inconsistency we introduced in Traefik's volume layout**:

The installer template has Traefik reading dynamic configs from `/data/dynamic` (a separate bind-mount):
```yaml
# installer template
- ${DATA_MOUNT}/traefik/traefik.yml:/etc/traefik/traefik.yml   # static config
- ${DATA_MOUNT}/traefik/dynamic:/data/dynamic:ro               # ← /data/dynamic in Traefik
- ${DATA_MOUNT}/traefik/acme.json:/data/acme.json
- ${DATA_MOUNT}/traefik/certs:/data/certs:ro
```

But the local setups now have Traefik reading from `/etc/traefik/dynamic` (because the whole volume is at `/etc/traefik`):
```yaml
# docker-compose.local.yml
- traefik-data:/etc/traefik                    # dynamic is at /etc/traefik/dynamic
```

The `traefik.yml` written by `init-traefik` reflects this: `directory: /etc/traefik/dynamic`. That's self-consistent locally, but diverged from what the installer does. That's fine — because the traefik.yml is always written by its respective bootstrap mechanism and hard-codes the correct internal path for that environment.

**The one thing that IS shared and must stay consistent**: `SANDCASTLE_TRAEFIK_CONFIG` always points to where **Rails** sees the file, which is always `/data/traefik/traefik.yml` — whether that's a host path (installer) or the named volume mounted there (local). That's consistent across all three compose files.

So the structures are intentionally different (volumes vs bind-mounts, init containers vs installer pre-bootstrap), and the Traefik internal path divergence is fine because the config file itself documents the correct internal path. Nothing is broken.

now deploy local does not have a mkcert cert any more!
https://dev.sand:8443/ 
DEBUG AND FIX!

content.thinking:
The issue is clear: Traefik is serving its default self-signed cert instead of the mkcert cert. This happened because I changed the Traefik volume mount from specific bind-mounts to the whole `traefik-data` volume at `/etc/traefik`. The mkcert cert was at `./.local/certs` and was previously mounted into Traefik at `/data/certs`, and Rails would reference it in `tls.yml` as `/data/certs/cert.pem`.

Now with the new structure:
- Traefik volume is at `/etc/traefik`
- I mounted `./.local/certs:/etc/traefik/certs:ro`


- But Rails is still writing cert paths to `tls.yml` based on environment variables that haven't been updated to match the new mount location, so Traefik can't find the actual cert files. So the `ensure_mkcert_cert` function generates certificates in `/data/traefik/certs`, which is bound to `./.local/certs` on the host, but `tls.yml` is trying to reference a path based on `SANDCASTLE_CERT_DIR` environment variable that might not match where the certs actually end up.

The mismatch is that Rails writes to `/data/traefik/certs` while the Traefik config expects them at `/data/certs` — and since Traefik's container has `./.local/certs` mounted at `/etc/traefik/certs`, it won't find the certificates at the path `tls.yml` is referencing. The issue is that `tls.yml` still references `/data/certs/` by default, but that path isn't mounted in Traefik anymore with my changes—I need to set `SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR=/etc/traefik/certs/` in the local compose file so the dynamic config points to where the certs actually are. the right path in Traefik's view.

In Traefik, the cert is at `/etc/traefik/certs/cert.pem`. So I need `SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR=/etc/traefik/certs` ... but that's the Traefik-internal path, and Rails doesn't know about that.

Actually the issue is simpler: `SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR` is supposed to be the **Traefik-perspective** cert path (where Traefik can find the cert). Rails uses `local_cert_dir` for writing, and `cert_dir` (=`SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR`) for the tls.yml reference.

So I need `SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR=/etc/traefik/certs` in the web container environment since that's where we mount `./.local/certs`. Rails writes certs to `/data/traefik/certs` (which is the same location bind-mounted), so if they're already there, the cert initialization will just find them and use them. The issue is that my new setup has the cert paths misaligned—Traefik reads from `/etc/traefik/certs` but the default `SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR` still points to `/data/certs`, which isn't mounted in Traefik. I need to set `SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR=/etc/traefik/certs` in the web container so the paths match up. the cert path mismatch is causing Traefik to fall back to its self-signed certificate. I need to check the dev configuration too—it looks like I changed the Traefik volume mount from `./.cert:/certs:ro` to `./.cert:/etc/traefik/certs:ro`, but didn't update the corresponding environment variable in the web container. I think option A is cleaner—keeping the cert mounts separate from the traefik-data volume means they won't need to change when paths shift. For the local setup, I'll mount the certs at `/data/certs` to match the default `SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR`, and do the same for the dev environment.

Wait, I'm realizing there's a conflict here. The local compose file has `SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: mkcert` but doesn't explicitly set `SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR`, so it defaults to `/data/certs`. But now that `traefik-data` is mounted at `/etc/traefik`, there's no `/data/certs` path available anymore—I need to rethink how the cert directory gets mounted alongside the main traefik data volume. I'm checking how the Ruby code writes certificates to `/data/traefik/certs/` which maps to `./.local/certs/` on the host, and Traefik needs to read from `/data/certs/` where the cert mount points, so restoring that bind-mount in the compose files should resolve the issue.

For the dev environment, I need to add back the cert volume mapping with the right path since `SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR: /certs` expects `./.cert:/certs:ro`, and for deploy:local, I accidentally removed the `./.local/certs:/data/certs` mount when I changed the Traefik volumes, which broke certificate access.

content.text:
The cert is there but Traefik can't find it. The `tls.yml` that Rails writes references `/data/certs/cert.pem` (default `SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR`) but I changed Traefik's cert mount from `./.local/certs:/data/certs` to `./.local/certs:/etc/traefik/certs` — breaking the path.

Minimal fix: restore the cert bind-mounts in Traefik at the paths that match `SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR`:

services:
  traefik:
    image: traefik:v3.3
    container_name: sandcastle-traefik
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "8080:80"
      - "8443:443"
      - "3000-3099:3000-3099"
    volumes:
      - traefik-data:/etc/traefik
      - ./.local/certs:/etc/traefik/certs:ro
    networks:
      - sandcastle-web
    depends_on:
      init-traefik:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  init-traefik:
    image: busybox
    volumes:
      - traefik-data:/data
    command:
      - sh
      - -c
      - |
        mkdir -p /data/dynamic /data/certs
        chown -R 220568:220568 /data
        if [ ! -f /data/traefik.yml ]; then
          cat > /data/traefik.yml << 'EOF'
        entryPoints:
          web:
            address: ":80"
          websecure:
            address: ":443"
        providers:
          file:
            directory: /etc/traefik/dynamic
            watch: true
        log:
          level: INFO
        EOF
        fi

  postgres:
    image: postgres:18
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql
      - ./docker/postgres/init-databases.sh:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init-databases.sh:ro
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: sandcastle
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
      POSTGRES_DB: sandcastle_production
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U sandcastle -d sandcastle_production"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  init-data:
    image: busybox
    volumes:
      - sandcastle-data:/data
    command: sh -c "mkdir -p /data/users /data/sandboxes && chown -R 220568:220568 /data"

  web:
    image: sandcastle:local
    build:
      context: .
      args:
        BUILD_VERSION: "${BUILD_VERSION:-dev}"
        BUILD_GIT_SHA: "${BUILD_GIT_SHA:-local}"
        BUILD_GIT_DIRTY: "${BUILD_GIT_DIRTY:-false}"
        BUILD_DATE: "${BUILD_DATE:-$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)}"
    container_name: sandcastle-web
    command: ["./bin/rails", "server", "-b", "0.0.0.0", "-p", "80"]
    group_add:
      - "${DOCKER_GID:-0}"
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      - sandcastle-data:/data
      - traefik-data:/data/traefik
      - ./.local/certs:/data/traefik/certs
    networks:
      - default
      - sandcastle-web
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: production
      RAILS_SHOW_DETAILED_ERRORS: "true"
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      SANDCASTLE_HOST: dev.sand
      SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR: /data
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: mkcert
      SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL: https://dev.sand:8443
      SANDCASTLE_TRAEFIK_CONFIG: /data/traefik/traefik.yml
      SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MIN: "3000"
      SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MAX: "3099"
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      init-data:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  worker:
    image: sandcastle:local
    build:
      context: .
      args:
        BUILD_VERSION: "${BUILD_VERSION:-dev}"
        BUILD_GIT_SHA: "${BUILD_GIT_SHA:-local}"
        BUILD_GIT_DIRTY: "${BUILD_GIT_DIRTY:-false}"
        BUILD_DATE: "${BUILD_DATE:-$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)}"
    container_name: sandcastle-worker
    command: ["bundle", "exec", "rake", "solid_queue:start"]
    group_add:
      - "${DOCKER_GID:-0}"
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      - sandcastle-data:/data
      - traefik-data:/data/traefik
    networks:
      - default
      - sandcastle-web
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: production
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      SANDCASTLE_HOST: dev.sand
      SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR: /data
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: mkcert
      SANDCASTLE_TRAEFIK_CONFIG: /data/traefik/traefik.yml
      SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MIN: "3000"
      SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MAX: "3099"
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      init-data:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  migrate:
    image: sandcastle:local
    command: ["./bin/rails", "db:prepare"]
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: production
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE:-deadbeef}
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER: thies
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_EMAIL: thieso@gmail.com
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD: tubu
      SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_SSH_KEY: "ssh-ed25519 REDACTED thieso@gmail.com"
      SANDCASTLE_SKIP_PASSWORD_CHANGE: "true"
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy

networks:
  sandcastle-web:
    external: true

volumes:
  pgdata:
  sandcastle-data:
  traefik-data:

content.text:
The cert flow for `local`:

```
mkcert writes cert   →  ./.local/certs/cert.pem  (host)
                              ↕ bind-mount
Rails reads/writes   →  /data/traefik/certs/cert.pem  (in web container)
tls.yml references   →  /data/certs/cert.pem           (SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR default)
                              ↕ bind-mount
Traefik reads cert   →  /data/certs/cert.pem           ✓
```

Both `./.local/certs:/data/traefik/certs` (web) and `./.local/certs:/data/certs:ro` (Traefik) point to the same host directory. The `traefik-data` volume at `/etc/traefik` handles `traefik.yml` and `dynamic/` — certs stay as a separate bind-mount at the path the existing `tls.yml` already knows about.

Restart Traefik to pick it up:
```bash
docker compose -f docker-compose.local.yml restart traefik
```
